title: gagea saxatilis subsp. szovitsii
intro: Gagea saxatilis subsp. szovitsii, also known as Szovits's Gagea, is a plant species belonging to the lily family and is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The medicinal properties of Szovits's Gagea include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its chemical compounds such as flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of this plant are said to include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate rheumatism. Herbal preparations of Szovits's Gagea are typically made by drying and processing its roots and flowers, which can be used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures for medicinal purposes.
